name: Fast Linting and Formatting with Ruff
description: Use Ruff to lint and format Python code at extreme speed, replacing Flake8, isort, Black, and dozens of other tools in a single binary.
trendingReason: Ruff has become the standard Python linter and formatter, adopted by major projects like FastAPI, Pandas, and Airflow for its speed and breadth of rules.

steps.title: Install Ruff
steps.description: Install the Ruff linter and formatter. It is a single Rust binary that runs 10-100x faster than Flake8 and Black combined.
steps.tip: You can also install via 'uv tool install ruff' or 'pipx install ruff' for a global installation.

steps.title: Lint your project
steps.description: Run the linter across all Python files in the current directory. Ruff supports 800+ lint rules from Flake8, isort, pyupgrade, and more.

steps.title: Auto-fix lint violations
steps.description: Automatically fix all safely fixable lint violations. Ruff will only apply fixes that do not change program behavior.
steps.warning: Review the changes after auto-fix. While safe fixes preserve behavior, they may alter code style in ways you want to verify.

steps.title: Format code (Black-compatible)
steps.description: Format all Python files using Ruff's built-in formatter, which is compatible with Black's output style. This replaces the need for a separate Black installation.

steps.title: Check formatting without modifying files
steps.description: Preview formatting changes without writing them. Useful in CI pipelines to enforce formatting standards without modifying code.
steps.tip: Use this command in your CI pipeline to fail the build if code is not properly formatted.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Ruff linting and formatting workflow
set -e

pip install ruff

# Add Ruff config to pyproject.toml if it exists
if [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then
  cat >> pyproject.toml << 'TOMLEOF'

[tool.ruff]
target-version = "py311"
line-length = 88

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "I", "N", "UP", "B", "SIM", "RUF"]
ignore = ["E501"]

[tool.ruff.format]
quote-style = "double"
indent-style = "space"
TOMLEOF
  echo "Ruff config added to pyproject.toml"
fi

echo "=== Linting ==="
ruff check . --fix

echo "=== Formatting ==="
ruff format .

echo "Linting and formatting complete."

faq.question: Does Ruff replace Black and isort?
faq.answer: Yes. 'ruff format' is a drop-in replacement for Black, and Ruff includes isort-compatible import sorting rules (I001, I002). You can remove Black, isort, and Flake8 from your dev dependencies.

faq.question: How do I configure which rules Ruff enforces?
faq.answer: Add a [tool.ruff.lint] section to pyproject.toml with 'select' for enabled rule prefixes and 'ignore' for specific rules to skip. Common starting sets include E (pycodestyle), F (pyflakes), I (isort), and UP (pyupgrade).

faq.question: Can I use Ruff with pre-commit hooks?
faq.answer: Yes. Add the ruff-pre-commit hook to your .pre-commit-config.yaml. Use 'ruff check --fix' for linting and 'ruff format' for formatting as separate hooks.
